text.title: writing-specifications Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for writing-specifications as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: "System should be fast" as a non-functional requirement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Not measurable. Rewrite as: "Response time < 500ms p95".

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Requirements written without personas are not grounded in user context and tend to describe system behavior rather than user needs.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unidentified requirements cannot be traced to design decisions or acceptance criteria.

failure-mode.description: Out of Scope section is empty or lists only obvious exclusions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Out of Scope must name specific features that were discussed and explicitly rejected; generic exclusions do not prevent scope creep.

failure-mode.description: AC items are not testable: "system should handle errors gracefully".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Acceptance criteria without Given-When-Then and specific values cannot be verified by a test.

failure-mode.description: Phase 1 (load context) requires .aidocs/constitution.md to exist; new projects stall if constitution is written in the same session.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Constitution writing requires understanding of tech stack constraints and design principles that must precede feature specification.

failure-mode.description: Socratic dialogue cannot be automated — user must be present for requirements elicitation phases.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Requirements extracted without stakeholder dialogue miss critical context and result in misdirected specs.

failure-mode.description: SMART criteria assessment requires domain knowledge that Haiku and fast-path agents lack.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Weaker models frequently mark vague requirements as SMART without catching subjective language.

failure-mode.description: Large specs (>300 lines) lose coherence; agents begin contradicting earlier sections.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Larger specifications exceed the context window effectively available for maintaining internal consistency.

failure-mode.description: "Out of Scope" is the hardest section to populate correctly — agents tend to leave it empty or list obvious exclusions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Defining what is explicitly NOT in scope requires business judgment and stakeholder agreement, not just feature analysis.
